data.narrative:
Xtend is a Series B-stage defense and security technology company that has raised $70M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 firms—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private—all operating in the same sector and stage. Five key private peers include Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $70M total raised proxy, implying a $133M valuation. The peer-median method examines six same-sector, same-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $1.0B to $1.5B, yielding a median of $1.2B. This wide disparity reflects material differences in revenue traction, customer concentration, or technology maturity across the peer set.

Synthesizing both approaches yields a valuation range of $113M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $666M. The 12x spread between methods signals that Xtend's position within the defense-tech maturity spectrum—whether earlier-stage or revenue-generating—is a critical determinant. Key uncertainties include customer diversification, defense contract pipeline visibility, and whether Xtend's technology or market access aligns more closely with $1B+ peers or earlier-stage cohorts. Further diligence on revenue run rate and customer retention would meaningfully narrow this range.
